answer_key.q1: A. Employees will be motivated if they feel belonged to the company.
answer_key.q2: E. If employees find their work lacking challenging, they will leave the company.
answer_key.q3: F. Employees will worry if their company is sold.

passages.title: Motivating Drives
passages.source_html:
Motivating Drives 


A 


Scientists have been researching the way to get employees motivated for many years. This research is a relational study which builds the fundamental and comprehensive model for study. This is especially true when the business goal is to turn unmotivated teams into productive ones. But their researchers have limitations. It is like studying the movements of car without taking out the engine. 


B 


Motivation is what drives people to succeed and plays a vital role in enhancing an organizational development. It is important to study the motivation of employees because it is related to the emotion and behavior of employees. Recent studies show there are four drives for motivation. They are the drive to acquire, the drive to bond, the drive to comprehend and the drive to defend. 


The Drive to Acquire 


C 


The drive to acquire must be met to optimize the acquire aspect as well as the achievement element. Thus the way that outstanding performance is recognized, the type of perks that are provided to polish the career path. But sometimes a written letter of appreciation generates more motivation than a thousand dollar check, which can serve as the invisible power to boost business engagement. Successful organizations and leaders not only need to focus on the optimization of physical reward but also on moving other levers within the organization that can drive motivation. 


The Drive to Bond 


D 


The drive to bond is also key to driving motivation. There are many kinds of bonds between people, like friendship, family. In company, employees also want to be an essential part of company. They want to belong to the company. Employees will be motivated if they find personal belonging to the company. In the meantime, the most commitment will be achieved by the employee on condition that the force of motivation within the employee affects the direction, intensity and persistence of decision and behavior in company. 


The Drive to Comprehend


E 


The drive to comprehend motivates many employees to higher performance. For years, it has been known that setting stretch goals can greatly impact performance. Organizations need to ensure that the various job roles provide employees with simulation that challenges them or allow them to grow. Employees don’t want to do meaningless things or monotonous job. If the job didn’t provide them with personal meaning and fulfillment, they will leave the company. 


The Drive to Defend 


F 


The drive to defend is often the hardest lever to pull. This drive manifests itself as a quest to create and promote justice, fairness, and the ability to express ourselves freely. The organizational lever for this basic human motivator is resource allocation. This drive is also met through an employee feeling connection to a company. If their companies are merged with another, they will show worries. 


G 


Two studies have been done to find the relations between the four drives and motivation. The article based on two studies was finally published in Harvard Business Review. Most authors’ arguments have laid emphasis on four-drive theory and actual investigations. Using the results of the surveys which executed with employees from Fortune 500 companies and other two global businesses (P company and H company), the article mentions about how independent drives influence employees’ behavior and how organizational levers boost employee motivation. 


H 


The studies show that the drive to bond is most related to fulfilling commitment, while the drive to comprehend is most related to how much effort employees spend on works. The drive to acquire can be satisfied by a rewarding system which ties rewards to performances, and gives the best people opportunities for advancement. For drive to defend, a study on the merging of P company and H company shows that employees in former company show an unusual cooperating attitude. 


I 


The key to successfully motivate employees is to meet all drives. Each of these drives is important if we are to understand employee motivation. These four drives, while not necessarily the only human drives, are the ones that are central to unified understanding of modern human life.

groups.title: Questions 27-40
groups.source_html:
Questions 27-31 


Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D. Write the correct letter in boxes 27-31 on your answer sheet.



27

According to the passage, what are we told about the study of motivation?

正确答案： C. Researchers have tended to be too theoretical to their study.

原文答案句：This research is a relational study which builds the fundamental and comprehensive model for study. But their researchers have limitations. It is like studying the movements of car without taking out the engine.

对应段落：A段

审题：题目问根据文章内容，关于对激励的研究我们了解到什么。

定位关键信息：利用题目中的"study of motivation"定位到A段首句"This research is a relational study..."和末句"But their researchers have limitations. It is like studying the movements of car without taking out the engine."。

解题思路：原文A段提到"researchers have limitations"并比喻为"studying the movements of car without taking out the engine"，说明研究者过于理论化，没有深入核心。这与选项C"Researchers have tended to be too theoretical to their study"意思一致。选项A文中未提及；选项B与原文"related to the emotion and behavior of employees"不符；选项D与原文"turn unmotivated teams into productive ones"不符。因此正确答案是C。


28

What can be inferred from the passage about the study of people’s drives?

正确答案： A. Satisfying employees’ drives can positively lead to the change of behaviour.

原文答案句：These four drives, while not necessarily the only human drives, are the ones that are central to unified understanding of modern human life.

对应段落：I段

审题：题目问从文章中关于人们的需求研究可以推断出什么。

定位关键信息：利用题目中的关键词"the study of people's drives"定位到文章中与需求研究相关的句子。

解题思路：根据I段最后一句"These four drives, while not necessarily the only human drives, are the ones that are central to unified understanding of modern human life."可以推断出满足员工的需求可以积极地改变他们的行为。原文中提到这四个需求对于理解现代人类生活至关重要，因此可以推断出满足这些需求会对员工的行为产生积极影响。


29

According to Paragraph C, in order to optimise employees’ performance, are needed.

正确答案： D. financial incentive and recognition

原文答案句：The drive to acquire must be met to optimize the acquire aspect as well as the achievement element. Thus the way that outstanding performance is recognized, the type of perks that are provided to polish the career path.  

对应段落：C段  

审题：题目问的是根据第三段，为了优化员工的表现，需要什么。  

定位关键信息：通过题目中的"optimise employees’ performance"和"paragraph three"定位到C段，找到与优化员工表现相关的句子。  

解题思路：原文提到"optimize the acquire aspect as well as the achievement element"（优化获取方面和成就要素），并进一步说明"the way that outstanding performance is recognized"（突出表现被认可的方式）和"the type of perks that are provided to polish the career path"（提供福利以完善职业道路）。选项D中的"financial incentive"（财务激励）对应"the drive to acquire"（获取的驱动力）和"perks"（福利），"recognition"（认可）对应"the way that outstanding performance is recognized"（突出表现被认可的方式），因此D选项最符合原文内容。


30

According to Paragraph E, how does 'the drive to comprehend' help employees perform better?

正确答案： B. It can help employees feel their task is meaningful to their companies.

原文答案句：Employees don’t want to do meaningless things or monotonous job. If the job didn’t provide them with personal meaning and fulfillment, they will leave the company.

对应段落：E段

审题：题目问的是根据第五段（E段），‘理解驱动力’如何帮助员工表现更好。

定位关键信息：通过题干中的‘the drive to comprehend’定位到E段，该段详细描述了理解驱动力对员工表现的影响。

解题思路：原文中提到，理解驱动力促使许多员工取得更高绩效。多年来，人们一直知道设定挑战性目标可以极大地影响绩效。组织需要确保各种工作角色为员工提供具有挑战性的刺激，或者允许他们成长。员工不想做没有意义的事情或单调的工作。如果工作没有给他们提供个人意义和成就感，他们就会离开公司。由此可知，理解驱动力帮助员工感觉他们的任务对公司有意义，因此选项B正确。


31

According to Paragraph F, which of the following is true about ‘drive to defend’?

正确答案： C. Employees think it is very important to voice their own opinions.

原文答案句：This drive manifests itself as a quest to create and promote justice, fairness, and the ability to express ourselves freely. The organizational lever for this basic human motivator is resource allocation. This drive is also met through an employee feeling connection to a company. If their companies are merged with another, they will show worries.

对应段落：F段

审题：题目问的是根据第六段（F段），关于‘drive to defend’（防御驱动力）的描述哪一项是正确的。

定位关键信息：题目中的关键词是‘drive to defend’，通过这个关键词可以定位到文章的F段，因为F段明确提到了‘drive to defend’。

解题思路：根据F段的内容，‘drive to defend’表现为追求创造和促进正义、公平以及自由表达自己的能力。组织可以通过资源分配来满足这种基本的人类动机，同时，这种驱动力也通过员工对公司产生联系来满足。如果他们的公司与其他公司合并，他们会表现出担忧。选项C提到员工认为自由表达自己的意见非常重要，这与F段中提到的‘drive to defend’的表现形式一致。因此，选项C是正确的。其他选项要么与原文不符，要么没有在原文中提到。


Questions 32-34 


Choose THREE letters, A-F. Write the correct letters in boxes 32-34 on your answer sheet.



Options (Word Bank):
A. Employees will be motivated if they feel belonged to the company.
B. If employees get an opportunity of training and development program, their motivation will be enhanced.
C. If employees’ working goals are complied with organisational objectives, their motivation will be reinforced.
D. If employees’ motivation is very low, companies should find a way to increase their salary as their first priority.
E. If employees find their work lacking challenging, they will leave the company.
F. Employees will worry if their company is sold.
32

Which THREE of the following statements are true of the study of drives?

正确答案： 1 A. Employees will be motivated if they feel belonged to the company.  |  2 E. If employees find their work lacking challenging, they will leave the company.  |  3 F. Employees will worry if their company is sold.

原文答案句：  

A. Employees will be motivated if they find personal belonging to the company.

E. Employees don’t want to do meaningless things or monotonous job. If the job didn’t provide them with personal meaning and fulfillment, they will leave the company.  

F. If their companies are merged with another, they will show worries.  

对应段落：  

A选项对应D段；E选项对应E段；F选项对应F段。  

审题：题目问的是关于研究驱动力（drives）的哪些陈述是正确的，需要选择三个正确的选项。  

定位关键信息：  

A选项中的"feel belonged to the company"对应D段中的"Employees will be motivated if they find personal belonging to the company"；  

E选项中的"lacking challenging"对应E段中的"Employees don’t want to do meaningless things or monotonous job. If the job didn’t provide them with personal meaning and fulfillment, they will leave the company"；  

F选项中的"company is sold"对应F段中的"If their companies are merged with another, they will show worries"。  

解题思路：  

A选项与D段内容一致，D段提到员工找到归属感时会受到激励，因此A正确；  

E选项与E段内容一致，E段提到如果工作缺乏挑战性，员工会离开公司，因此E正确；  

F选项与F段内容一致，F段提到公司合并时员工会表现出担忧，因此F正确。其他选项B、C、D在原文中未明确提及或与原文不符。


Questions 35-40 


Do the following statements agree with the claims of the writer in Reading Passage 3? In boxes 35-40 on your answer sheet, write YES if the statement agrees with the claims of the writer NO if the statement contradicts the information NOT GIVEN if it is impossible to say what the writer thinks about this.



35

Increasing pay can lead to the high work motivation.

正确答案： B. NO

原文答案句：Successful organizations and leaders not only need to focus on the optimization of physical reward but also on moving other levers within the organization that can drive motivation.

对应段落：C段

审题：题目问“增加工资可以导致高工作动力”是否与作者的观点一致。

定位关键信息：利用题干中的关键词“Increasing pay”和“high work motivation”在文章中定位相关句子。

解题思路：原文中提到成功的组织和领导者不仅需要关注物质奖励的优化，还需要关注其他能驱动动力的因素。这表明作者认为增加工资（物质奖励）不能单独提高高工作动力，还需要其他因素。因此，题目中的说法与作者的观点不符，答案为NO。


36

Local companies benefit more from global companies through the study.

正确答案： C. NOT GIVEN

原文答案句：Using the results of the surveys which executed with employees from Fortune 500 companies and other two global businesses (P company and H company), the article mentions about how independent drives influence employees’ behavior and how organizational levers boost employee motivation.

对应段落：G段

审题：题目问的是“本地公司通过研究从全球公司中获益更多”这一说法是否与作者的观点一致。

定位关键信息：根据题目的关键词"Local companies", "benefit", "global companies", "study"，在文章中定位相关信息。

解题思路：文章G段提到“Using the results of the surveys which executed with employees from Fortune 500 companies and other two global businesses (P company and H company), the article mentions about how independent drives influence employees’ behavior and how organizational levers boost employee motivation.”，但并未提及本地公司通过研究从全球公司中获益更多。因此，无法确定作者是否认为本地公司通过研究从全球公司中获益更多。答案为NOT GIVEN。


37

Employees achieve the most commitment if their drive to comprehend is met.

正确答案： B. NO

原文答案句：Employees will be motivated if they find personal belonging to the company. In the meantime, the most commitment will be achieved by the employee on condition that the force of motivation within the employee affects the direction, intensity and persistence of decision and behavior in company.

对应段落：D段

审题：题目问的是“如果员工的‘理解需求’得到满足，他们就能达到最大的承诺。”

定位关键信息：题目中的“drive to comprehend”和“most commitment”是关键词，通过这两个词可以定位到文章中D段的相关句子。

解题思路：原文中D段明确提到，员工如果能在公司找到归属感，就能被激励。而题目中说的是“理解需求”得到满足时，员工能达到最大的承诺。原文中并没有提到“满足理解需求”就可以实现“最大承诺”。因此，题目中的说法与原文信息不符，故答案为NO。


38

The employees in former company presented unusual attitude toward the merging of two companies.

正确答案： A. YES

原文答案句：For drive to defend, a study on the merging of P company and H company shows that employees in former company show an unusual cooperating attitude.

对应段落：H段

审题：题目问的是“前公司的员工对两家公司的合并表现出异常的态度。”

定位关键信息：利用题目中的“former company”和“unusual attitude”定位到H段。

解题思路：原文中明确提到，对于防御驱动力的研究，涉及P公司和H公司的合并，结果显示前公司的员工表现出异常的合作态度。这与题目中的描述完全一致，因此答案为YES。


39

The two studies are done to analyse the relationship between the natural drive and the attitude of employees.

正确答案： B. NO

原文答案句：The two studies have been done to find the relations between the four drives and motivation.

对应段落：G段

审题：题目问的是“这两项研究是为了分析自然驱动力和员工态度之间的关系吗？”

定位关键信息：根据题目中的“two studies”和“natural drive”和“attitude of employees”可以定位到G段的第一句话。

解题思路：原文中提到的研究是为了找出“four drives”和“motivation”之间的关系，而题目中说的是“natural drive”和“attitude of employees”之间的关系。原文中的“four drives”指的是B段中提到的四个驱动力，即“the drive to acquire, the drive to bond, the drive to comprehend and the drive to defend”，而题目中的“natural drive”并没有明确的指代，可能指的是这四个驱动力，也可能不是。另外，原文中说的是“motivation”，而题目中说的是“attitude of employees”，这两个概念并不完全相同。因此，题目中的陈述与原文中的信息不符，所以答案是NO。


40

Rewarding system cause the company to lose profit.

正确答案： C. NOT GIVEN

原文答案句：Successful organizations and leaders not only need to focus on the optimization of physical reward but also on moving other levers within the organization that can drive motivation.

对应段落：C段

审题：题目问的是奖励系统是否会导致公司失去利润。

定位关键信息：通过题目中的关键词“Rewarding system”和“lose profit”在文章中定位答案。

解题思路：原文中提到成功的组织和领导者不仅需要关注物质奖励的优化，还需要关注其他可以驱动动机的组织杠杆。原文并没有提到奖励系统会导致公司失去利润，因此无法得出题目所述的结论。所以，答案为NOT GIVEN。
